headline: Final Year MEng CS @ Imperial | Ex-Amazon, DOJO
about:
more info at: mohamedsharif.tech

My goal is to help build something millions of people use and love. Think Notion, Figma, Claude. The opposite is imagine your workplace and you’re forced to use the most outdated, cumbersome, hateful app for your payroll, or ticketing or communication. That’s where I want to stay as far away from

experience.description:
Now building Proposer - an AI Legal Mediation System. It uses a hybrid RAG + Knowledge Graph architecture to predict tribunal outcomes and facilitate fair settlements, no lawyers required. With Glass-Box reasoning to understand fully why a judge might land on a settlement, as well as rational mediation to help propose satisfiable outcomes for both landlords and tenants.

Prev: Building an AI memory layer integrated with your browser so that when you use your favourite chatbot, it has automatic context from the content you've researched. (Parked)

Engaged with prospective students from diverse backgrounds, promoting STEM fields and inspiring the new scientific brightest minds during summer schools and open days.
Conducted academic sessions as well as university support sessions in various different areas and subjects.
Presented at educational institutions as well as honed essential skills in communication, teamwork, organization, and marketing.

Worked on large-scale AI training workloads at Amazon Web Services (between S3 and Pytorch) where I optimised the AWS S3 Connector for PyTorch to speed up distributed ML training jobs.
Benchmarked performance using Python/Boto3, analysing latency & throughput to guide design improvements.
Scaled distributed checkpointing to support workloads from 80 instances to 300+ EC2 instances (1M+ S3 requests/min).
Built a “shadow copy” system that reduced S3 5xx errors by 90%, making training pipelines more reliable.
Added error-rate analysis + logging dashboards to pinpoint I/O bottlenecks in large training jobs.
Analysed model-training patterns and FSDP sharding behaviour from AI Labs to improve data-loading efficiency and training reliability.

1.3k community members

>30 events

> few thousand attendees

organized 2 24-hour hackathons





...nearly burnt out

Delivered high-impact features across Dojo’s merchant ecosystem, working on C# .NET backend services, GraphQL APIs, and React frontends.
Core contributor to Hardware Care, a new flagship revenue stream projected to generate £10.5M+ per year.
Designed and implemented key workflows for pricing, eligibility, and back-book customer rollout.
Demonstrated strong ownership by driving major feature delivery during a period where 60–70% of the team was on holiday, handling blockers, coordinating with dependencies, and ensuring deadlines were met.
Collaborated with product, design, and leadership, culminating in presentations to senior execs including the CEO.
Gained deep experience in designing customer-facing financial products, large-scale system behaviour, and shipping high-quality software under real-world constraints.

Delivered programming content to children in primary schools in different applications such as Scratch and p5.js, allowing me to gain experience in explaining concepts in a simple and concise way as well as to inspire creativity into upcoming computer science enthusiasts.

Achieved significant contributions to successful product launches for the new NHS SystmOne and played a pivotal role in guiding 95% of our team's product developments at Vodafone.
    Collaborated closely with the Product Owner to simplify processes and tackle complex challenges, leveraging technical skills to analyze intricate issues.
    Led sprint planning, daily stand-ups, and retrospectives, driving iterative development by working closely with diverse teams in different companies.
    Streamlined the development process by translating business requirements into user stories and acceptance criteria.

Collaborated with a diverse team to propose a sustainable product for potential investment and conducted research on the effects of the cost of living on investment firms.
Through analysis of different datasets and using our skills in Python and SQL, we helped advise
data-driven decisions that would provide valuable insights to guide the firm's strategies.

I took part in the research placement where I analyzed the aerodynamics and performance of an aero-engine compressor. The project involves the use of an in-house axial-flow compressor performance simulation software to model, simulate a compressor blading at specific operating conditions.
I took on the part of improving the GUI of the compressor (written in Python) and fixing errors.
